summary: End-to-end testing of VORTEX AI MVP. Backend pytest: 21/21 passing covering auth (signup/login/me/duplicate/invalid), forgot+reset flow, chat send + conversation continuation + persistence, conversations CRUD, per-user isolation (User B cannot see/access User A's convs), dashboard summary, logout. Frontend Playwright covered Landing render, Signup -> /dashboard with welcome name, sidebar (chat/profile/settings/logout) visible, chat input + loading indicator, protected route redirect (logged-out /dashboard -> /login), admin login -> /dashboard, /forgot reachable. Fixed two blocker bugs during testing: (1) Signup endpoint returned 500 (Mongo _id ObjectId leaked into response) -- patched server.py. (2) Frontend webpack-dev-server overlay covered the whole page due to unknown ESLint rule 'react-hooks/set-state-in-effect' in src/lib/auth.jsx and src/pages/ChatPage.jsx -- removed those disable comments. After fixes, full E2E flow works; only a single transient 502 on /chat/send was observed (LiteLLM internal retry timed out at ingress) -- backend logs show retry pattern, retried sends in pytest all returned 200. Frontend handles failure with toast + optimistic rollback (verified visually).

minor.issue: Occasional 502 from ingress when LiteLLM internally retries the Anthropic call (>30s). Recommend: tune backend timeout/retry or surface a streaming response. Not blocking -- subsequent sends succeed.

action_items: Optional: increase backend httpx/LLM timeout or add a server-side retry-with-shorter-prompt fallback so transient Anthropic latency doesn't bubble up as 502 to the user.
Optional: remove the 'react-hooks/set-state-in-effect' eslint-disable lines from any future code (the rule isn't registered in this project's ESLint config).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: signup originally returned the raw inserted dict including Mongo _id (now patched). Apply same defensive '_id' exclusion to any future endpoint that returns post-insert docs.
server.py is 539 lines and could be split (auth, chat, conversations, billing, admin) into modules; manageable but approaching split threshold.
logout for JWT users only deletes user_sessions row; JWT tokens remain valid until natural expiry (7 days). Acceptable for MVP, but a token blocklist would be better for true logout.
Frontend Landing uses unsplash/pexels remote images -- harmless, but consider self-hosting for offline reliability.
ProtectedRoute relies on AuthProvider's initial /auth/me check -- verified working (redirects to /login when logged out).
test_credentials: Admin admin@vortex.ai / VortexAdmin@2026 (seeded); e2e signups use e2e_user_<hex>@vortex-ai.com
seed_data_creation: Test users created via /api/auth/signup with TEST_/e2e_ prefixed emails; not cleaned up (free credits only, low cost).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite is the regression baseline at /app/backend/tests/test_vortex_api.py. Note: Pydantic EmailStr rejects '.test' TLD -- use '@vortex-ai.com' or similar in tests. Frontend was blocked by a CRA webpack overlay due to an unknown eslint rule; if you ever see all elements interception-blocked by '#webpack-dev-server-client-overlay', check eslint errors first. AI chat takes 3-15s; allow generous timeouts on /chat/send.
rca_of_the_issue: Signup 500: motor's insert_one mutates the supplied dict adding _id (ObjectId). The endpoint returned that dict as the 'user' field; FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize ObjectId -> ValueError -> 500. Fix: exclude '_id' (and 'password_hash') from the response dict. Frontend overlay: package's ESLint config does not register 'react-hooks/set-state-in-effect'; CRA elevates eslint errors to a full-page overlay iframe that intercepts pointer events. Fix: remove the orphan eslint-disable comments.
